raw_text:
Prism Analytics Inc.

Turning customer data into actionable insights that drive growth

Now Hiring: Lead Product Manager

San Francisco, CA · Full-time · $185,000 - $245,000

J

oin Prism Analytics as a Lead Product Manager to own and evolve our core analytics platform while

mentoring a team of 4-5 product managers and working cross-functionally with engineering and design

leadership. In this leadership role, you'll drive product strategy for our flagship dashboarding and segmentation

suite, oversee the delivery of quarterly roadmaps, and cultivate a data-driven product culture across the

organization. You'll be responsible for defining success metrics using tools like Amplitude and Mixpanel,
conducting discovery with enterprise customers, and ensuring your product team operates with clarity, impact, and

autonomy.

A B O U T U S

Prism Analytics Inc. is a B2B SaaS company that helps mid-market and enterprise organizations unlock the power

of their customer data through advanced analytics and real-time behavioral insights. Founded in 2018, we've

grown to serve over 500 enterprise customers across verticals including fintech, e-commerce, and martech. Our

platform integrates with existing data ecosystems to provide product teams with cohort analysis, funnel

visualization, and predictive user segmentation—enabling data-driven product decisions at scale.

Y O U R R O L E

Lead and mentor a team of 4-5 product managers, providing coaching on discovery methodologies, cross-

functional collaboration, and customer-centric thinking; conduct regular 1-on-1s and facilitate team

retrospectives to drive continuous improvement

Own the product strategy and roadmap for the analytics platform, including dashboarding, real-time alerts, and

cohort management features; define quarterly OKRs and ensure alignment with company strategy

Champion user research and discovery practices, conducting customer interviews, usability testing sessions,

and competitive analysis to inform product decisions; synthesize insights into clear requirements for

engineering partners

Define, instrument, and monitor product success metrics using Amplitude and Mixpanel; establish northstar
metrics, retention curves, and engagement funnels to track feature adoption and platform health

Partner with VP of Engineering and Head of Design to scope complex initiatives, manage trade-offs, and

ensure successful delivery; lead design reviews, technical architecture discussions, and cross-team planning

sessions

 Develop SQL-based analytics queries (or work with analytics engineers) to perform deep-dive analyses on user

behavior, feature usage, and performance anomalies; generate data-driven insights to support product decisions

Represent the product team in executive discussions and board updates; communicate product vision, progress,
and impact to stakeholders; prepare quarterly business reviews that highlight key metrics and strategic

initiatives

Establish product standards, documentation, and playbooks for your team; implement structured feedback

loops from customers, sales, and support teams to inform product iterations

Build and maintain relationships with 5-10 strategic customers; present product roadmaps, gather feedback on
planned features, and advocate for critical customer needs within product prioritization

R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

6+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years in a leadership capacity managing product

managers, associate product managers, or product analysts

Demonstrated experience with analytics, product intelligence, or data infrastructure platforms; strong

familiarity with concepts like cohort analysis, funnel analysis, event tracking, and user segmentation

Proficiency with analytics tools such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar; ability to set up events, build

dashboards, and interpret complex datasets to drive product decisions

Strong SQL skills; comfort writing queries to validate hypotheses, segment users, or investigate product

anomalies; experience translating SQL results into actionable insights

Track record of shipping products to market and measuring their impact; portfolio examples demonstrating
how your product decisions directly influenced metrics like retention, engagement, or revenue

Excellent communication and storytelling skills; ability to articulate product vision, rationale, and results to

executives, engineers, designers, and customers at varying levels of technical sophistication

Experience working in cross-functional environments with engineering and design teams; proven ability to

build trust, navigate trade-offs, and influence without direct authority

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Experience building or shipping B2B SaaS products, particularly in analytics, data, or BI categories

Familiarity with modern data stack tools (Snowflake, Looker, Tableau) and APIs used in product analytics

integrations

Background in technical product management or a technical degree (computer science, mathematics, or related

field)

Experience mentoring and developing junior product managers or cross-functional team members; familiarity
with coaching frameworks or product management methodologies

Exposure to ML/AI use cases within product analytics or predictive personalization

Experience scaling product teams or navigating rapid company growth in the growth or Series B/C stage

 T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Amplitude, Mixpanel, SQL (PostgreSQL/Snowflake), Looker, Jira, Figma, Google Analytics 4, Segment, Linear

(or Azure DevOps), Slack

W E O F F E R

Competitive equity package with standard 4-year vesting and 1-year cliff

Comprehensive health coverage including medical, dental, vision, and mental health support with company
contribution of up to 85% of premiums

Unlimited PTO with a clear expectation of 25+ days annually; additional paid sabbatical eligibility after 5 years

Professional development budget of $3,500/year for courses, conferences, certifications, and coaching

Flexible work policy with ability to work remotely or from our San Francisco HQ; quarterly in-person team

offsites

Parental leave (16 weeks primary caregiver, 12 weeks secondary caregiver) at 100% pay

401(k) plan with 4% company match; automatic enrollment after 30 days

Annual performance bonus targeted at 20-25% of base salary based on company and individual goals

T O A P P LY

Apply directly through our careers page at careers.prismanalytics.com or send your resume, cover letter, and a

brief description of a product decision you made using analytics to hiring@prismanalytics.com. Please include 2-3
examples of dashboards or analyses you've commissioned to support product decisions. Interviews will include a

discovery discussion with our VP of Product, a team lunch with product managers, and a systems design exercise

focused on instrumentation and metrics strategy.
